_readme: Upcoming events shown on the home page (list + calendar) and the
Calendar page. Edited from the /admin page (or by hand) until Ludus
embed/API access exists.
Each event needs: title and date (YYYY-MM-DD, used for sorting and for
placing it on the calendar grid). Once its date has passed an event
moves out of the upcoming list into the Calendar page's collapsible
'Past events' archive (and the month grid pages back to it) - nothing
needs deleting. Backfill historic events from the live Google Calendar
with tools/import-calendar.mjs (run from the droplet).
Optional: url (the Ludus event page - without it the event shows
with no Tickets link), time (e.g. '7:00 PM'), presenter (who's
putting it on), dateLabel (display text that overrides the
formatted date, for runs like 'July 18-20'), and description (the
event blurb as Markdown, mirroring the live calendar's blurb with
its HTML converted to Markdown; not yet rendered on the page).
Enter a multi-day run as one entry per day with the same title and
dateLabel: every day gets marked on the calendar, and the list
collapses them into one.
Keys starting with _ are ignored by the site.
If this list is empty or unreachable, the pages simply fall back to
the 'browse events on our ticketing site' link - nothing breaks.
The entries below mirror the live site's calendar as of 2026-07-03:
the eventscalendar.co widget on bonitacenterforthearts.com, which is
fed by the public Google Calendar (bonitacenter@gmail.com). Titles
are kept verbatim; descriptions are the live calendar's blurbs
with their HTML converted to Markdown; school-district entries
(First Day of School, breaks) are included
because the live calendar shows them. Keep this in sync via /admin.

events.title: GREASE presented by Bonita High School
events.description:
Since its electric Broadway and West End debut in the early 1970’s, GREASE has remained one of the world’s most popular and enduring musicals. Funny, frank and featuring the hit songs “Greased Lightnin’,” “Hopelessly Devoted to You,” “You’re The One That I Want,” and “Summer Nights,” GREASE follows the journey of Danny and Sandy, alongside the Burger Palace Boys and the Pink Ladies, as they navigate high school to the unforgettable rock n’ roll soundtrack that defined generations.
Tickets $10 in advance at the Bonita High School Finance office (located at 3102 D St, La Verne, CA 91750) or prior to each performance at the Bonita Center for the Arts.

events.title: Merano Cultural Performances presents the Grand Opening Ceremony of the 4th American "Inheritance Cup" International Arts Festival
events.description:
**Speak to the world through global connections! **Art is to stay together in all directions. On August 7, 2021, the grand opening ceremony of the 4th American "Inheritance Cup" International Arts Festival will be held at the American Performing Arts Theater-San Dimas, Bonita Center for the Arts, Address: 822 W Covina Blvd, San Dimas, CA 91773, used stage art to promote the great "anti-epidemic spirit" of the new era, and paid tribute to all anti-epidemic heroes by performing the first performance.

The concert was hosted by the Los Angeles County Government of the United States and the Merano Culture and Performing Arts Group of the United States, and organized by the Chinese Musicians Association of the United States and the Overseas Opera Union, sending a sincere voice to the world through a global synchronization connection.

The concert performance program is divided into three chapters-"Most Beautiful Retrograde", "Heroes Salute", "Flowers and Full Moon", which are composed of theme recitations, music performances, song singing, and opera performances, which are used as tribute to "anti-epidemic" The hero's theme concert led the audience to immerse the audience in the particularly memorable moment of "All the people fighting the epidemic" in Los Angeles. In the comprehensive interpretation of various types of programs, the artists will express their exquisite true feelings to immerse the audience in the wonderful stage art. At the same time, we deeply felt the mission spirit of “being courageous and willing to contribute” during the fight against the epidemic, the unity spirit of helping each other in the same boat, the spirit of daring to fight and winning, respecting the law, seeking truth and being pragmatic. The spirit of science and the spirit of sharing a common destiny and overcoming difficulties together."

The concert specially invites government officials from the Los Angeles County government who have participated in the front-line "anti-epidemic", the cutest people of our time-front-line medical staff, police officers from the police station, fire-fighting heroes from the Los Angeles Fire Department, elementary and middle school faculty, and their families A total of more than a thousand people came to watch the performance. Los Angeles County Sheriff Catherine Bagh, Public Health Director Barbara Feller, Los Angeles County Police Chief Alex Villanueva and other government officials will give speeches on the evening of the performance. The concert's chief director Director Sophie said: “The return of the theater will undoubtedly rekindle the enthusiasm for life of the general public, inject precious positive energy into the economic recovery of Los Angeles, and it is also a major landmark sign of the full return of citizens’ lives to normal order. This is a clear stand: everything we had in normal life in the past must be returned one by one. The performance in this special period is not easy to come by under the premise of many epidemic prevention and control requirements. I hope that each of us can cherish it. This is a hard time coming."

 "Being able to watch the first performance of the re-enactment with the anti-epidemic heroes feels more meaningful than watching the performance before, and the mood is particularly complicated." Mr. Li Yan, president of the Merano Cultural Performing Arts Group of the United States, highly praised the performance: "So this time. The performance with heart and affection must be wonderful and moving, especially when you see the video clips of the anti-epidemic period being played on the big screen. Everyone is clear about the past experience as yesterday, and naturally sighs, and it brings the time back to the period of national anti-epidemic A special day to stick to. Fortunately, as the offline rehearsal of the theater is gradually in full swing, people's lives are gradually returning to normal, and we are extremely looking forward to it."

The opening ceremony of the festival will be reported by the American CBS Television Network, NBC Television Network, ABC Television Network, FOX Television Network and CNN mainstream media.

[FOR MORE INFORMATION](https://www.meranocp.com/contactus)

TICKET INFORMATION AVAILABLE AFTER JULY 20TH

events.description:
Parallel **Live Broadcast Closing Ceremony: The** 2021 4th American Heritage Cup International Arts Festival will be held on August 21 at the San Dimas Theater in Los Angeles. The exclusive live channel of the American Chinese Musicians Association and the US City TV 18.9 will be paralleled to live broadcast the closing ceremony and awards ceremony of the 4th American Heritage Cup International Art Festival, which will be conducted continuously from 19:00 to 22:00 that night.

As a special guest of honor, a member of the United States Congress, supporting and guiding the Chinese Consulate General in Los Angeles, special guests include political officials, leaders of overseas Chinese, political and business celebrities, previous "Inheritance Cup" gold medal winners, contemporary drama performance artists, contemporary opera Famous artists, local American musicians, Hollywood filmmakers, and winners of this year participated in the closing ceremony.

events.title: Little Shop of Horrors presented by Bonita High School Drama Dept featuring LIVE BAND
events.description:
**_Don’t feed the plants!_**

Meek floral assistant Seymour Krelborn stumbles across a new breed of plant he names “Audrey II”- after his coworker crush.  This raucous , R&B-singing carnivore promises unending fame and fortune to the down and out Krelborn as long as he keeps feeding it, BLOOD.  Over time, Seymour discovers Audrey II’s out of this world origins and intent towards global domination!

Featuring stunning puppetry and favorite hit songs like “Suddenly Seymour”, “Skid Row” and “Somewhere That’s Green”, Bonita High School Drama Department, Music Department and Vocal Department collaborate to make this production of _Little Shop of Horrors_ one you won't soon forget!

events.title: Pilipinx American Cultural Night: "Magkita Tayo Muli"
events.description:
Ready to get immersed into Pilipinx culture? This year, Cal Poly Pomona's Barkada is holding its 32nd annual Pilipinx American Cultural Night which will be hosted at the Bonita Center for the Performing Arts. This year's production "Magkita Tayo Muli" combines elements of theatre, song and dance as main character Rose Evangelista rediscovers her roots and rebuilds her connection with her estranged family in the Philippines. This show was curated by Culture Chair Ethereal Violet Reyes and Barkada's Entertainment Company Coordinator Robechelle Mina."

events.title: "Matilda Jr." Presented by Ramona Middle School
events.description:
Matilda JR is the story of an extraordinary little girl with extraordinary powers. The daughter of abusive parents, Matilda finds refuge in library books, which she reads quickly and in creating her own stories. Things are no better at school, where Matilda also must face a tyrannical and cruel headmistress, Miss Trunchbull, who believes in Crunchem Hall’s motto: “Bambinatum est Magitum,” or “Children are Maggots.” She finds companionship in her teacher, Miss Honey, who, though well-intentioned, is shy and fragile. Brave little Matilda knows she has to stand up against the adults in her world, and in doing so, discovers her own remarkable powers. Matilda’s bravery teaches Miss Honey and her classmates an important lesson -- that even though life can be hard, “nobody but me is gonna change my story” so “sometimes you have to be a little bit naughty.”

**[GET TICKETS](https://bonitacenterforthearts.ludus.com/17414)**

events.title: Nrityodaya Academy Performance
events.description:
Kathak Dance is one of eight forms of Indian classical dance. This dance style can trace its origins all the way back to the nomadic bards of ancient northern India, known as Kathakars or storytellers. The name Kathak dates back thousands of years to the ancient Sanskrit language and translates to “Story” and a Kathaka in Sanskrit means roughly “he or they who tells a story.” This beautiful and graceful dance style has been capturing the hearts and elating the senses of people for centuries. Nrityodaya Kathak Academy and School of Dance has continued to pass this ancient traditional dance down to the next generation of “Kathakars” or storytellers.

To learn more visit [socalkathakdan](http://socalkathakdance.com)ce.com[](http://socalkathakdance.com)

events.title: The Wolves: A Bonita High School Drama Production
events.description:
Seating for this show is General Admission.

_Mature Themes and Language_

In order to preserve the intimate nature of the performance, seating is onstage and around the performers which is a departure from our traditional seating layout; as a result, seating is first-come, first served.  We will never oversell seats, though you will want to arrive early if you wish to sit with a group.

Featuring an all-female cast (comprised of nine adolescents and one briefly-seen adult), The Wolves chronicles six Saturday mornings in the lives of a soccer team somewhere in suburban America as they prepare for their games. The group of girls, whom at first seem indistinguishable as each is referred to by only her number and all are clad in the same jersey only bearing her number, quickly become identifiable and different. In the course of six short weeks, the Wolves deal with love, loss, and identity in ways that real teenagers do.

**[GET TICKETS](https://bonitacenterforthearts.ludus.com/20042)**

_Tickets will be LIVE at midnight on 10/10._

Celebration of Life: Aidan Kocalis
events.description:
Aidan Thomas Kocalis (Peterson), “Bubba”, passed away unexpectedly at the age of 25, at home, in his sleep, early on the morning of Saturday, August 12, 2023. Aidan lived in Upland, CA, and was a friendly delivery driver for Domino’s Pizza in Rancho Cucamonga/Alta Loma, CA–easily recognizable in his yellow convertible Mustang, jamming to his heavy metal and punk tunes. (Continued [HERE](https://www.hilgenfeldmortuary.com/obituaries/aidan-thomas-kocalis-peterson/))

events.title: "Inspired By Black: Student Reflections on African American Artistry"
events.description:
**San Dimas High School Presents "Inspired By Black: Student Reflections on African American Artistry"**

San Dimas High School is proud to announce the upcoming _"Inspired By Black: Student Reflections on African American Artistry,"_ an exhibition showcasing the creative work of over 40 talented student artists inspired by the work of Black and African American artists. From the mixed media portraits of Delita Martin to the dynamic paintings of Ernie Barnes and the groundbreaking conceptual installations of Senga Nengudi, students have created works in a diverse range of styles, themes, media, and cultural influences.

The exhibition will take place at the Bonita Center for the Arts throughout February, and features drawings, paintings, sculptures, and mixed media pieces. This show offers the community the opportunity to engage and reflect on the work of Black and African American artists, both historical and contemporary. By presenting these student artworks, the exhibition highlights the enduring legacy and influence of Black artistry while fostering dialogue about its critical role in shaping art history and culture.

Visual and Performing Arts Department chair and art teacher, E. Dominic Black said, “All of the student artists selected a Black artist whose work spoke to them, and each created an original piece which reflects on the artist’s style, technique, or subject matter. I am proud of their efforts and the integrity they showed in researching and honoring another artist. The student works represent all levels of the San Dimas art program, and the show is going to be incredible.”

The "Inspired By Black" art show addresses a critical issue in the art world: the historic exclusion of many talented artists from mainstream art history. This systemic oversight can be attributed to several interconnected factors, including Eurocentrism and institutional gatekeeping. Additionally, the intersection of race and class created obstacles that many artists of color were unable to overcome during their lifetimes.

In recent decades, scholars, curators, and educators have worked to rectify these inequities, spotlighting underrepresented voices and revising the narratives that have shaped our understanding of art. "Inspired By Black" is a continuation of these efforts, providing students the opportunity to study, honor, and reimagine the works of African American artists.

San Dimas High School 10th grader Addy Andersen selected Wesley Clark as her inspiration.  She said, “Wesley Clark not only makes visual art, but he also makes historical references and social commentary on issues that can't be described in words, issues that go beyond a canvas, exposing truths about race, power, and systemic oppression in America. After researching Clark, I gained a respect for him and his activism, and his techniques. Every word I used in my original artwork was inspired by his works, and every detail represents his legacy.”

A public celebration for the student artists will be held on **Thursday, February 20, from 4 to 8 PM **at the Bonita Center for the Arts. Attendees will have the chance to meet the student artists, hear about their creative processes, and participate in this cultural celebration.

**An Invitation to the Community** San Dimas High School invites the community to experience this exciting exhibition. The show not only highlights the creativity and dedication of its students, but also underscores the importance of equity and inclusivity in the arts. Visitors will have the opportunity to explore a diverse range of artwork, all accompanied by artist statements that explain the students' processes and inspirations.

For more information, please contact:
**E. Dominic Black, MA Ed****
****Visual Arts Department Chair, SDHS****
****[d.black@bonita.k12.ca.us](mailto:d.black@bonita.k12.ca.us)****
****909-971-8230 x3210**

**About San Dimas High School****
**San Dimas High School is committed to fostering creativity, diversity, and critical thinking among its students. Through events like the "Inspired By Black" art show, the school aims to provide enriching educational experiences that empower young artists to engage with culture and history in meaningful ways.

events.title: Teacher of the Year Event
events.description:
The La Verne and San Dimas Chambers of Commerce are proud to announce the inaugural Teacher of the Year Awards, a collaborative celebration recognizing the outstanding contributions of educators in our communities. This marks the first time both cities have joined together to honor teachers who make a profound impact on our students, families, and the local community. 

The event will take place on Tuesday, May 6, 2025, at the Bonita Center for the Arts, beginning with a mocktail hour at 4:00 p.m., followed by the program at 5:00 p.m. 

The evening will include a special ceremony to showcase the dedication, innovation, and hard work of teachers who go above and beyond to inspire and empower their students. The Teacher of the Year Awards will be a memorable evening celebrating the passion, commitment, and lasting influence of exceptional educators. Teachers from La Verne and San Dimas will be honored for their ability to inspire their students, colleagues, and the broader community.

events.title: Nritya Swara: Melody in Motion
events.description:
**For any ticket inquiries, please contact Deeya Narayanan at +1 (909) 635-8436**Nrityo Swara: Melody in Motion is a vibrant Kathak dance production presented by the Nrityodaya Kathak Academy, under the artistic direction of Bhairavi Kumar. This event celebrates the elegance and storytelling tradition of Kathak. The show features a special performance by the legendary Pt. Rajendra Gangani, a maestro of the Jaipur gharana.

Accompanied by a live ensemble of classical musicians—including tabla virtuosos Fateh Singh Gangani and Mayookh Bhaumik, vocalist Hemant Panwar, sarangi maestro Pt. Pankaj Mishra, sitarist Rajib Karmakar, vocalist Aditya Ranade, and flautist Sujeet Phanse—the performance promises a rich confluence of rhythm and melody.

Experience the magic of a truly immersive performance, where the delicate interplay of rhythm and melody transports you to a world of emotion, culture, and timeless beauty.

Doors Open at 4pm, program begins 4:30pm

events.description:
Theaward-winning Bearcat Singers bring their voices back to the Bonita Center forthe Arts for Bonita High School’s Annual Fall Concert. This year’s programfeatures an inspiring and dynamic selection of music performed by more than 200singers from BHS Choral Music’s combined choirs. Experience the artistry ofChorale, Women’s Choir, Men’s Choir, Concert Choir, and Chamber Singers, underthe direction of Mr. Ryan Yoder, Director of Choral Music, with AssistantDirector Alexis Ohmar. Don’t miss the chance to celebrate the power, passion,and joy of live music with our incredible Bearcat Singers at this year’s FallConcert!

Tickets: $10

events.title: Masters of Harmony present "Merry & Bright - Celebrate the Holidays in Harmony!"
events.description:
Celebrate the season with the Masters of Harmony at **Merry & Bright: Celebrate the Holidays in Harmony!** The curtain rises at 3 PM on Sunday, December 7th at the Bonita Center for the Arts in San Dimas.

Get ready for heart-warming holiday favorites and a few brand-new tunes from the nine-time international champions who know how to make spirits merry and bright! 

Our friends in SoCal Mix will join the festivities, and we’re thrilled to welcome back the unforgettable Satin Dollz — bringing their signature 1940s flair and toe-tapping energy to the stage.
 

Reserve seats today and let the holidays begin in harmony!

**[GET TICKETS!](https://mastersofharmony.ludus.com/200504264)**
